title: Splinter of the Mind's Eye 2

'''''Splinter of the Mind's Eye'' 2''' is the second issue of [[Dark Horse Comics]]'s [[Star Wars: Splinter of the Mind's Eye (comic series)|''Star Wars: Splinter of the Mind's Eye'' comic adaptation]] of the novel ''[[Splinter of the Mind's Eye]]''. It begins where [[Splinter of the Mind's Eye 1|''Splinter of the Mind's Eye'' #1]] left off—a few pages into the fourth chapter—and ends approximately halfway through the eighth chapter of the book.

==Publisher's summary==
''Imprisoned by the Imperial forces, confronted by two very violent, very massive creatures known as Yuzzem, Luke and Leia have only one hope of escape: the Mimban Crystal, capable of giving its wielder total, omnipotent mastery of the Force. But Luke's use of the crystal could draw the unwelcome attention of certain … ''dark'' powers''.

==Plot summary==
{{Quote|Maybe he'll be generous. Good workers are tough to get here. He may leave you most of your fingers…|A stormtrooper, regarding Captain-Supervisor Grammel.|<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 2">[[Splinter of the Mind's Eye 2|''Splinter of the Mind's Eye'' 2]]</ref>}}
Luke Skywalker, his sister Leia, and their assailants are arrested by Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] for the crime of "fighting with weaponry in a public place." Luke and Leia are taken directly to [[Captain-Supervisor]] [[Grammel]], who believes them to be common criminals but suspects them to be something more after being informed of the mysterious shard of crystal that Luke was given by [[Halla]].

While Luke and Leia are assigned to a cell containing two Yuzzem, Grammel, intrigued by the shard, contacts the [[Moff/Legends|regional governor]], [[Bin Essada/Legends|Bin Essada]], who is known for his knowledge of radiant stones. When Essada sees the pictures of the two "petty criminals" caught in possession of the gemstone, he recognizes the female and orders Grammel to hold them in custody unharmed until the "proper authority" arrives on [[Mimban/Legends|Mimban]] to collect them.

Luke, Leia and the two Yuzzem stage a daring escape from the Imperial prison and then meet up with Halla and the droids to steal a [[swamp crawler]] that they plan to use to reach the Temple of Pomojema—and the Kaiburr crystal. However, a hungry [[wandrella]] has other plans for them.

==Continuity==
The comic adaptation of ''Splinter of the Mind's Eye'' remained fairly faithful to its source material. Because of the requirements of the format and the limited space in which to tell the story, a number of elements were either altered or omitted, however. The departures from the book included:

*In the book, the sergeant who commands the stormtroopers who break up the fight between Luke, Leia and the miners is distinguished by a series of three angular marks on his helmet and his armored sleeve.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 82">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye'', page 82 (Chapter 4)</ref> In the comic he bears no markings resembling those described in the book. Instead, he wears an orange pauldron.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 2 3">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye #2'', page 3</ref>

*In the book, as Luke and Leia are escorted to an imposing-looking ziggurat, Luke asks Leia where they are going. Leia responds that they are going to the prison and Luke says that he's impressed.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 83">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye'', page 83 (Chapter 4)</ref> In the comic, the dialogue of this exchange is nearly the same, but Luke's question is answered by the sergeant instead of Leia.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 2 3" />

*Grammel's office is described as being luxuriously furnished in the book.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 86">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye'', page 86 (Chapter 4)</ref> In the comic, the office is sparsely furnished with equipment to one side of Grammel's desk and a number of power lines dangling from the ceiling.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 2 4">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye #2'', page 4</ref>

*In the book, Luke has kept the Kaiburr crystal shard inside the small lockbox that Halla originally had it in. After Luke gives the box with the shard inside to Grammel, Grammel asks Luke for the combination—which Luke doesn't know.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 102">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye'', page 102 (Chapter 5)</ref> In the comic, Luke appears to not have kept the shard inside the lockbox. When he decides to give Grammel the shard, he simply gives the shard to Grammel.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 2 7">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye #2'', page 7</ref>

*The screen with which Grammel contacts Bin Essada in the book is hidden within the wall behind his desk.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 108">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye'', page 108 (Chapter 5)</ref> In the comic, the screen is contained within the desk.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 2 9">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye #2'', page 9</ref>

*In order to show Essada the images of Luke and Leia that his recording rod captured, Grammel holds the instrument before the [[vis pickup]] on his end in the book.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 111">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye'', page 111 (Chapter 5)</ref> The way in which Essada is allowed to see the images in the comic is apparently achieved by a different, but unknown, method, given that the images of Luke and Leia are shown independently of Grammel's on Essada's end of the transmission.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 2 11">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye #2'', page 11</ref>

*The only indications that Essada will contact Darth Vader are an ambiguous reference by Essada to an "interested party," which was not meant to be heard by Grammel, and Grammel's reference to an "Imperial representative" when he talks to Luke and Leia in their cell.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 112">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye'', page 112 (Chapter 5)</ref><ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 122">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye'', page 122 (Chapter 6)</ref> The comic makes this less ambiguous by showing Essada contacting Vader with his news after finishing his conversation with Grammel.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 2 11" />

*In the book, shortly after he escapes from the prison with the others in the mud crawler, Luke charges his lightsaber with the blaster that he used during the prison break.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 155">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye'', page 155 (Chapter 7)</ref> In the comic, Luke is shown doing this seven days after the prison escape, shortly before the wandrella attack.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 2 21">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye #2'', page 21</ref>

*In the book, Grammel's doctor tells him that he will never sweat from his rebuilt arm.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 157">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye'', page 157 (Chapter 8)</ref> In the comic, she adds that his hair will never grow back.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 2 20">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye #2'', page 20</ref>

*During the wandrella attack in the book, Hin and Kee attack the wandrella with a pair of blaster rifles that they had appropriated from the swamp crawler's storage space.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 164">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye'', page 164 (Chapter 8)</ref> In the comic, the two Yuzzem use a mounted turret that is never mentioned in the book's description of the swamp crawler.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye 2 23">''Splinter of the Mind's Eye #2'', page 23</ref>
